entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company that should prioritize building persistent, objective talent profiles

entities.name: Pure Talent profile
entities.context: Honest, universally applicable candidate profile created from data collected independent of specific job applications

entities.name: Observer effect
entities.context: Social science principle stating individuals modify behavior when they know they are being studied

entities.name: Application incentive
entities.context: Distorting force in hiring where candidates optimize for specific job requirements, obscuring true profile

entities.name: Persistent talent profiles
entities.context: Long-term data assets more predictive of success across multiple roles than traditional resumes

entities.name: Hiring market
entities.context: Domain where independent candidate data collection yields higher fidelity than application-based collection

relationships.context: Should prioritize building persistent profiles rather than just processing applications
Observer effect explains why application incentive distorts candidate behavior
Application incentive opposes the creation of pure talent profiles by introducing performance distortion
Pure Talent profiles form the basis for persistent, objective talent data
